title: AUD/USD Outlook Q2 2026
summary: Analysis of AUD/USD momentum heading into Q2 driven by RBA policy and China demand.
content: The Australian dollar faces crosscurrents as RBA dovish signals compete with improving Chinese demand for commodities. We expect AUD/USD to trade in the 0.6500-0.6850 range through Q2.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: AUD/USD Technical Levels to Watch
summary: Key support and resistance zones for AUD/USD in the near term.
content: AUD/USD is consolidating near the 0.6700 level after failing to break above 0.6800. The 200-day moving average at 0.6650 provides critical support. A break below opens the door to 0.6550.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: AUD/GBP: UK Fiscal Policy Impact on Cross Rate
summary: How upcoming UK budget decisions may influence AUD/GBP dynamics.
content: The UK government faces challenging fiscal choices that could weigh on sterling, benefiting AUD/GBP. Potential tax increases and spending cuts may slow UK growth, while RBA remains relatively steady.
author: Emily Carter

title: AUD/CNH and the PBOC Policy Framework
summary: Examining how PBOC monetary policy shifts affect AUD/CNH dynamics.
content: With the PBOC gradually easing policy to support growth, AUD/CNH faces a complex outlook. The wide rate differential with Australia and iron ore trade flows remain key drivers for the cross.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD/GBP Cross Rate Opportunity
summary: Relative value opportunity in AUD/GBP as macro fundamentals diverge.
content: Australian economic data has outperformed the UK recently, creating a tactical long AUD/GBP opportunity. Commodity strength vs UK services weakness favors AUD. We target 0.5450 with a stop at 0.5180.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: AUD/USD: Iron Ore Correlation Deep Dive
summary: Reassessing the AUD-iron ore relationship as Chinese demand patterns evolve.
content: The historical correlation between AUD/USD and iron ore prices remains strong at 0.82 in 2026. Chinese steel production recovery and infrastructure spending continue to drive both AUD and iron ore.
author: Emily Carter

title: RBA Watch: April 2026
summary: Preview of upcoming RBA meeting and expected policy decision impact on AUD pairs.
content: April features the RBA decision with markets pricing a 60% chance of a hold. We outline scenario analysis for AUD/USD, AUD/CNH, and AUD/GBP under different rate outcomes.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: AUD/CNH: Navigating Trade Policy Risks
summary: How evolving China-Australia trade policies affect the AUD/CNH outlook.
content: Trade policy uncertainty continues to create volatility in AUD/CNH. We analyze tariff scenarios on Australian exports and PBOC management of the daily fix as key drivers for the pair.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: Volatility Analysis: AUD Pairs Options Surface
summary: Options market signals for AUD/USD, AUD/CNH, and AUD/GBP.
content: Risk reversals across AUD pairs show mixed signals. AUD/USD 1-month 25-delta risk reversal at -0.8, suggesting hedging against AUD downside. AUD/CNH skew elevated on China risk. AUD/GBP vol compressed near cycle lows.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: AUD/GBP: BoE-RBA Policy Divergence Play
summary: Rate differential shift between BoE and RBA creating tactical opportunity in AUD/GBP.
content: BoE signaling potential rate cuts while RBA maintains cautious stance. The narrowing rate differential and Australia commodity strength support a tactical long AUD/GBP position targeting 0.5420.
author: Emily Carter

title: AUD/CNH: China Stimulus Impact Assessment
summary: How recent Chinese fiscal stimulus affects the AUD/CNH cross rate.
content: The CNY 2 trillion stimulus package creates competing forces for AUD/CNH. While it boosts Australian export demand (AUD positive), it also strengthens CNH through growth optimism. Net effect is range-bound near 4.70.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD/GBP Breakout Watch
summary: AUD/GBP approaching a multi-month range breakout level.
content: AUD/GBP has been compressing in a 0.5180-0.5380 range for six weeks. Momentum indicators suggest a breakout is imminent. A close above 0.5380 targets 0.5500; below 0.5180 opens 0.5050.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: Quarterly AUD Forecast Update
summary: Updated forecasts for AUD/USD, AUD/CNH, and AUD/GBP through Q4 2026.
content: We revise our AUD/USD forecast to 0.6600 by year-end from 0.6900 previously. AUD/CNH is adjusted to 4.65. AUD/GBP target raised to 0.5450. Full forecast table included.
author: Emily Carter

title: AUD/USD: China Stimulus Hopes Lift Australian Dollar
summary: The Aussie rallied to 0.6580 as Beijing announced a broad fiscal stimulus package, boosting risk appetite and commodity demand. Further upside eyed toward 0.6650.
content:
AUD/USD advanced 0.6% to 0.6580 on Thursday, its highest level since January, after China's State Council unveiled a CNY 2 trillion fiscal stimulus package targeting infrastructure and domestic consumption.

Key Drivers:

  • China's CNY 2 trillion stimulus package boosted risk sentiment globally.
  • Iron ore prices rose 3.5% to $115/tonne, supporting the Australian dollar.
  • RBA minutes struck a balanced tone, not pre-committing to further cuts.

Technical Outlook:

The break above the 0.6550 resistance zone is constructive. The next resistance is at 0.6620, followed by 0.6650. Support is at 0.6520.

Trading Recommendation: Long AUD/USD on pullbacks to 0.6540 targeting 0.6650, stop below 0.6490.
author: Sarah Lin

title: AUD/USD Technical Analysis: Key Resistance at 0.6800
summary: AUD/USD is testing major resistance at 0.6800. A decisive break could signal broader AUD strength across all crosses.
content:
AUD/USD is at a crossroads. After a 3% rally from early-February lows, the pair is testing the 0.6800 level.

Technical Picture:

  • Resistance: 0.6800 (key level), 0.6880 (200-week MA)
  • Support: 0.6650 (50-day MA), 0.6550 (trend support)
  • RSI (14): 58 - approaching but not yet overbought
  • MACD: Positive crossover in place but momentum slowing

Scenario Analysis:

Bullish AUD Scenario (40%): A clean break above 0.6800 opens the path to 0.6950.

Bearish AUD Scenario (60%): Rejection at 0.6800 and reversal below 0.6700 would signal the end of the corrective rally.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: AUD Impact from Fed Pivot Timeline Shift
summary: Stronger US data has pushed the first Fed rate cut expectation back to September 2026. We examine how this shift impacts AUD/USD, AUD/CNH, and AUD/GBP.
content:
The repricing of Fed expectations following a string of upside US economic surprises has materially shifted the first cut expectation from June to September 2026.

Impact by Pair:

  • AUD/USD: Fed-RBA rate differential widening pressures AUD/USD lower. Chinese stimulus provides an offsetting positive. Range 0.6500-0.6800.
  • AUD/CNH: Both PBOC and RBA in easing mode. Cross remains driven primarily by commodity trade flows. Neutral near 4.68.
  • AUD/GBP: BoE likely to cut before Fed, supporting AUD/GBP upside. Target 0.5400.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: AUD/CNH: PBOC Easing Cycle Benefits Australian Exporters
summary: PBOC rate cuts and RRR reduction are weakening CNH broadly, pushing AUD/CNH higher as Australian commodity exports remain in demand.
content:
AUD/CNH rallied to 4.7500 on Thursday as the PBOC cut the MLF rate by 10bps and signaled further easing measures ahead.

Key Drivers:

  • PBOC MLF rate cut to 2.40% from 2.50%, weakening CNH.
  • Iron ore imports from Australia rose 5% MoM, supporting bilateral trade flows.
  • RBA holding rates steady provides relative yield support for AUD.

Technical Outlook:

AUD/CNH has broken above the 4.7200 resistance. Next target is 4.7800, followed by 4.8200. Support at 4.7000.

Trading Recommendation: Long AUD/CNH on dips to 4.7200 targeting 4.7800, stop below 4.6800.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD/GBP Rallies as UK CPI Undershoots
summary: AUD/GBP surged to 0.5350 after UK CPI printed below consensus, raising expectations of an early BoE rate cut while RBA holds steady.
content:
AUD/GBP advanced 0.8% on Wednesday after official data showed UK consumer price inflation eased more than anticipated to 2.8% year-on-year, boosting BoE cut expectations and weakening GBP.

Key Drivers:

  • UK CPI fell to 2.8% YoY, core CPI dropped to 3.5% from 3.7%.
  • Money markets now price a 70% probability of a May BoE rate cut.
  • RBA maintaining neutral stance, creating positive rate differential for AUD.

Technical Outlook:

AUD/GBP broke above the 0.5300 resistance. Next target at 0.5400, followed by 0.5450. Support now at 0.5280.

Trading Recommendation: Long AUD/GBP on pullbacks to 0.5300 targeting 0.5450, stop below 0.5250.
author: Emily Carter

title: AUD/CNH: China Property Sector Risks and Trade Flow Analysis
summary: Chinese property sector stress creates mixed signals for AUD/CNH. While CNH weakens on capital outflows, Australian commodity demand may also soften.
content:
AUD/CNH is consolidating in a narrow 4.6500-4.7200 range as markets weigh competing forces from China's property sector difficulties.

Key Drivers:

  • Chinese property developers facing renewed liquidity stress, weighing on CNH.
  • Steel demand declining 3% QoQ, reducing iron ore import volumes from Australia.
  • PBOC intervening to stabilize yuan, limiting CNH downside.

Technical Outlook:

The pair is forming a rectangle pattern. A break above 4.7200 targets 4.7800; below 4.6500 opens path to 4.6000.

Trading Recommendation: Neutral - wait for range resolution before initiating positions.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: Fundamental Analysis: Why AUD/USD Could Break Above 0.7000 in Q2 2026
summary: A confluence of fundamental tailwinds - China stimulus, commodity prices, and RBA pause - presents a structural case for AUD/USD to reclaim levels not seen since early 2025.
content:
Looking beyond near-term noise, a structural case is building for a sustained AUD/USD rally toward 0.7000 by the end of Q2 2026.

1. China Stimulus Creates Commodity Demand Tailwind

The CNY 2 trillion infrastructure package is directly boosting demand for Australian iron ore, coal, and LNG exports.

2. Terms of Trade at Multi-Year Highs

Australia terms-of-trade index rose 3.2% as export prices outpace import costs.

3. RBA Rate Pause Provides Yield Support

With the RBA signaling data-dependence, AUD retains carry advantage vs several G10 peers.

12-Month Target: AUD/USD 0.7000. Build long positions on dips below 0.6600.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: AUD/GBP Weekly Technical Note
summary: Preliminary technical analysis of AUD/GBP ahead of the upcoming BoE and RBA decisions.
content: AUD/GBP is testing the upper boundary of its 6-week consolidation range ahead of key central bank meetings. RSI divergence suggests momentum building for a breakout above 0.5380.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: AUD/CNH: Rare Earth Trade Tensions Analysis
summary: China's rare earth export restrictions creating new dynamics for AUD/CNH trading. We assess the impact on bilateral trade flows and currency cross.
content:
China's decision to restrict rare earth exports has added a new dimension to AUD/CNH analysis. While Australia is developing its own rare earth mining capabilities, the near-term impact on trade flows is uncertain.

Key Drivers:

  • China controls 60% of global rare earth production and 90% of processing.
  • Australia has the world's 6th largest rare earth reserves.
  • Export restrictions may accelerate Australian rare earth development, long-term AUD positive.

Trading Recommendation: Neutral near-term, bullish AUD/CNH on 6-12 month horizon as Australian alternatives develop.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD Weekly Review: All Three Crosses
summary: Weekly performance review of AUD/USD, AUD/CNH, and AUD/GBP with outlook for the week ahead.
content:
AUD had a mixed week across the three key crosses. AUD/USD gained 0.4% on China stimulus, AUD/CNH fell 0.2% on PBOC intervention, and AUD/GBP rose 0.6% on weak UK data.

Week Ahead:

  • RBA minutes release on Tuesday - key for AUD/USD direction.
  • China trade data on Wednesday - impacts AUD/CNH.
  • UK employment data on Thursday - drives AUD/GBP.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: AUD/USD: Australian Employment Data Preview
summary: Preview of this week Australian employment report and potential AUD/USD scenarios.
content:
Australian employment data due Thursday is expected to show 25K jobs added. A beat above 40K could push AUD/USD above 0.6800 resistance, while a miss below 10K risks a test of 0.6550 support.

Scenario Analysis:

  • Strong beat (40K+): AUD/USD to 0.6850, AUD/CNH to 4.78, AUD/GBP to 0.5400.
  • In-line (20-30K): Limited reaction, ranges maintained.
  • Miss (below 10K): AUD/USD to 0.6550, AUD/CNH to 4.62, AUD/GBP to 0.5200.
author: Emily Carter
